utility_evaluation.REASONING: Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 top-level items. Now check if each is fully specified according to what should be covered with costs: 1) health_coverage: requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Provided plan lists physical and mental plans with monthly costs—fully specified. 2) property_coverage: requires home/contents insurance with costs. Plan provides home_contents_insurance with monthly cost—fulfilled. 3) travel_coverage: requires travel insurance with costs. Provided with monthly cost—fulfilled. 4) additional_services: requires any enhancement services with costs. Plan sets it to "N/A" with no costs—this is not fully specified as per requirement to include services with costs; however, if none exist, N/A might be acceptable, but the prompt asks to include enhancement services with costs, so it's not fully specified. 5) total_budget: requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown—both monthly and annual costs provided—fulfilled. Therefore, 4 out of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists four items: EliteCare Premium Plan for physical health, LifeFirst Standard Plan for mental health, EliteHome Premium Coverage for home contents, and PrimeTravel Premium Plan for travel. I matched each item name exactly to the corresponding entries in the ground-truth ratings and extracted their numeric ratings.
